meaning: n.网孔,网丝,网眼,圈套,陷阱,[机]啮合vt.以网捕捉,啮合,编织vi.落网,相啮合
examples: If the cogs don't mesh correctly, the gears will keep slipping.
The mesh structure is particularly obvious on the Internet.
The different rods, placed at a height of two meters, shape a continuous mesh that "cuts down" the height of the establishment.
They sleep with the windows open, the moths battering their wings against the mesh screens.
Loop quantum gravity can be visualised, as its name suggests, as a mesh of loops.
